####based on instructions from karyoploteR: https://bernatgel.github.io/karyoploter_tutorial//Examples/PlotGenes/PlotGenes.html
and adapted from: https://github.com/TC-Hewitt/OatCrownRust
##prepare a regions table from genome with total chromosome lengths
module load samtools
samtools faidx mygenome.fasta
printf "chr\tstart\tend\n" > mygenome.granges.txt
TAB='\t'
cut -f1,2 mygenome.fasta.fai | sed "s/${TAB}/${TAB}0${TAB}/g" >> mygenome.granges.txt
##prepare regions of interest to be plotted along the chromosomes, called loci.granges.txt. Can be converted from gff3.
##consists of chr, start, end, name, colour
awk -F'\t' 'BEGIN{OFS="\t"}
  $0 !~ /^#/ && $3=="gene" {
    id=".";
    if (match($9, /ID=[^;]+/)) { id=substr($9, RSTART+3, RLENGTH-3) }
    print $1, $4, $5, id, "#2C7BB6"
  }' genes.gff3 > loci.granges.txt

#####This part is in R
library(karyoplotR)
library(svglite)

mygenome <- toGRanges("mygenome.granges.txt")
loci <- toGRanges("loci.granges.txt")
hapFG <- c("chr1F", "chr1G", "chr2F", "chr2G", "chr3F", "chr3G", "chr4F", "chr4G", "chr5F", "chr5G", "chr6F", "chr6G", "chr7F", "chr7G", "chr8F", "chr8G", "chr9F", "chr9G", "chr10F", "chr10G", "chr11F", "chr11G", "chr12F", "chr12G", "chr13F", "chr13G", "chr14F", "chr14G", "chr15F", "chr15G", "chr16F", "chr16G", "chr17F", "chr17G", "chr18F", "chr18G")
svglite("chrsB.svg", width=8, height=6)

kp_hapFG <- plotKaryotype(genome=mygenome, chromosomes=hapFG)
kpPlotRegions(
    kp_hapFG, loci, 
    data.panel="ideogram", 
    r0=NULL, r1=NULL, 
    col=loci$colour, 
    border='#000000', 
    avoid.overlapping = FALSE)
